At $99 upfront plus $120/year for full features, the real sticker price is higher than it looks.
This isn’t a spec upgrade.
Our ChatGPT vs Gemini 2026 breakdown shows how fast Gemini’s share of real-world AI usage has climbed — from 7.27% to 26.7% of generative AI traffic in under a year.
Here’s the catch: Gemini Live, Camera History Search, and Home Briefs all require Google Home Premium — $10/month for Standard, $20/month for Premium.
$99 upfront plus $10/month after the trial = $219 in year one, $120/year ongoing.
Google Home Speaker — Amazon Echo Dot Max — Apple HomePod mini
If you’re switching from Alexa or Apple, the migration cost — rebuilding routines, re-pairing devices, learning a new app — often isn’t worth it just for better conversational AI."},{"url":"https://mashable.com/tech/google-home-speaker-2026-gemini-announced","title":"The first Google Home Speaker in years has Gemini, costs $99, and arrives soon | Mashable","content":"The new Berry color is fresh.

The device costs $99 and comes in four colors: Hazel, Porcelain, Jade, and Berry.
The news from Google also confirms a leak from earlier this month, though now we have additional details and photos.
On the plus side, users who already have a subscription to Google AI Pro or Ultra subscription plans will automatically get access to Google Home Premium at no extra cost.

{"query":"Google Home Speaker built for Gemini launch 2026 specs pricing availability","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.techtimes.com/articles/319111/20260626/google-home-speaker-goes-sale-gemini-runs-device-best-features-cost-extra.htm","title":"Google Home Speaker Goes on Sale: Gemini Runs On-Device, Best Features Cost Extra","content":"#### Now on sale at $99.99, Google’s Gemini speaker runs AI on-device but best features need a $10 monthly plan. The Google Home Speaker went on sale June 25, 2026, six years after the Nest Audio and seven after the Nest Mini — two devices that are now discontinued. Priced at $99.99, the speaker is the first Google audio hardware built from the ground up for the Gemini AI assistant, and it carries an architectural change that distinguishes it from every Google smart speaker that came before: a dedicated Neural Processing Unit that runs Gemini Nano inference locally on the device, eliminating the cloud round-trip that defined all prior Nest speakers. The practical result is response times Google describes as under 100 milliseconds for standard voice commands — and a meaningful shift in the privacy picture for always-on listening devices, at a moment when Google reached a $68 million class action settlement in January 2026 over prior speakers' recording of private conversations.","score":0.8660535,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SgBy3NFGl3U","title":"The Smart Home Speaker You Can Talk to Like a Real Person: Google Home Speaker (2026)","content":"The Smart Home Speaker You Can Talk to Like a Real Person: Google Home Speaker (2026)

The 2026 Google Home Speaker is built around Gemini, and for the first time, talking to a smart speaker should actually feel natural. At around $100, the speaker isn’t super-expensive. Just know that the best features cost extra.
